title: Carbocyclic prolinamide derivatives
abstract: This invention is directed to novel carbocyclic prolinamide derivatives of Formula (I), and pharmaceutically acceptable salts, solvates, solvates of the salt and prodrugs thereof, useful in the prevention (e.g., delaying the onset of or reducing the risk of developing) and treatment (e.g., controlling, alleviating, or slowing the progression of) of age-related macular degeneration (AMD) and related diseases of the eye. These diseases include dry-AMD, wet-AMD, geographic atrophy, diabetic retinopathy, retinopathy of prematurity, polypoidal choroidal vasculopathy, and degeneration of retinal or photoreceptor cells. The invention disclosed herein is further directed to methods of prevention, slowing the progress of, and treatment of dry-AMD, wet-AMD, and geographic atrophy, diabetic retinopathy, retinopathy of prematurity, polypoidal choroidal vasculopathy, and degeneration of retinal or photoreceptor cells, comprising: administration of a therapeutically effective amount of compound of the invention. The compounds of the invention are inhibitors of HTRA1. Thus, the compounds of the invention are useful in the prevention and treatment of a wide range of diseases mediated (in whole or in part) by HTRA1. The compounds of the invention are also useful for inhibiting HTRA1 protease activity in an eye or locus of an arthritis or related condition.
claims:
Claims WHAT IS CLAIMED IS: 1. A compound of Formula I: (I) or a pharmaceutically acceptable salt, solvate, solvate of the salt or prodrug thereof wherein: W is selected from the group consisting of:ГўВЂВ“B(OH) 2 andГўВЂВ“C(O)C(O)NR 7 R 8 ; R 1 is selected from the group consisting of: (a) ГўВЂВ“(CH 2 ) 0-6 -aryl, and (b) ГўВЂВ“(CH 2 ) 0-6 -heteroaryl, wherein the aryl and heteroaryl of choices (a) and (b) are each optionally substituted with 1 to 3 substituents independently selected from the group consisting of: (i) ГўВЂВ“halogen, (ii) ГўВЂВ“CN, (iii) ГўВЂВ“C 1-6 alkyl, (iv) ГўВЂВ“C 0-6 alkyl-R 6 , (v) ГўВЂВ“C 2-6 alkenyl, (vi) ГўВЂВ“C 2-6 alkynyl, (vii) ГўВЂВ“C(O)R 7 , (viii) ГўВЂВ“CO 2 R 7 , (ix) ГўВЂВ“CONR 7 R 8 , (x) ГўВЂВ“OH, (xi) ГўВЂВ“O-C 1-6 alkyl, (xii) ГўВЂВ“O-C 0-6 alkyl-R 6 , (xiv) ГўВЂВ“S(O) p -C 1-6 alkyl, (xv) ГўВЂВ“S(O) p -C 0-6 alkyl-R 6 , (xvi) ГўВЂВ“S(O) 2 NR 7 R 8 , (xvii) ГўВЂВ“NO 2 , (xviii)ГўВЂВ“NR 7 R 8 , (xix) ГўВЂВ“NHC(O)R 7 , (xx) ГўВЂВ“NHC(O)OR 7 , (xxi) ГўВЂВ“NHC(O)NR 7 R 8 , (xxii) ГўВЂВ“NHSO 2 C 1-6 alkyl, and (xxiii)ГўВЂВ“NHSO 2 C 0-6 alkyl-R 6 , (xxiv)ГўВЂВ“CONH(CH 2 ) 2-4 -[O(CH 2 ) 2-4 ] m OC 1-4 alkyl, wherein each of the alkyl group of choices (iii), (iv), (xi), (xii), (xiv), (xv), (xxii), (xxiii) and (xxiv) is optionally substituted with 1 to 5 substituents independently selected fromГўВЂВ“ halogen,ГўВЂВ“haloC 1-4 alkyl,ГўВЂВ“COR 7 ,ГўВЂВ“CO 2 R 7 ,ГўВЂВ“CONR 7 R 8 ,ГўВЂВ“NR 7 R 8 ,ГўВЂВ“OH,ГўВЂВ“O-C 1-4 alkyl,ГўВЂВ“SH and -S-C 1-4 alkyl; R 2a and R 2b are independently selected from the group consisting of: (a) ГўВЂВ“H, (b) ГўВЂВ“C 1-8 alkyl, and (c) ГўВЂВ“C 0-6 alkyl-R 6 , wherein each of the alkyl group of choices (b) and (c) is optionally substituted with 1 to 5 substituents independently selected from: (i) ГўВЂВ“halogen, (ii) ГўВЂВ“haloC 1-4 alkyl, (iii) ГўВЂВ“NR 7 R 8 , (iv) ГўВЂВ“OH, (v) ГўВЂВ“O-C 1-4 alkyl, (vi) ГўВЂВ“SH, (vii) ГўВЂВ“S-C 1-4 alkyl, (viii) ГўВЂВ“NR 7 SO 2 C 1-4 alkyl, (ix) ГўВЂВ“NR 7 C(O)R 7 , and (x) ГўВЂВ“NR 7 C(O)OR 7 ,

A pharmaceutical composition comprising a compound of any of Claims 1-11 and a pharmaceutically acceptable carrier. 13. A method of preventing, or treating a disease of the eye selected from dry-AMD, wet-AMD, geographic atrophy, diabetic retinopathy, retinopathy of prematurity, polypoidal choroidal vasculopathy, and degeneration of retinal or photoreceptor cells, comprising: administering to a subject in need thereof a therapeutically effective amount of a compound according to Claims 1-11 or a pharmaceutically acceptable salt, solvate, solvate of the salt or prodrug thereof or the pharmaceutical composition of Claim 12. 14. The method of preventing a disease of the eye according to Claim 13 wherein the method of prevention is selected from delaying the onset of disease and reducing the risk of developing a disease of the eye, wherein the disease of the eye is selected from dry-AMD, wet-AMD, and geographic atrophy, diabetic retinopathy, retinopathy of prematurity, polypoidal choroidal vasculopathy, and degeneration of retinal or photoreceptor cells. 15. The method of treating a disease of the eye according to Claim 13 wherein the method is selected from controlling, alleviating, and slowing the progression of, wherein the disease is selected from dry-AMD, wet-AMD, and geographic atrophy, diabetic retinopathy, retinopathy of prematurity, polypoidal choroidal vasculopathy, and degeneration of retinal or photoreceptor cells. 16. The method according to any of Claims 13 to 15 wherein the disease is geographic atrophy. 17. A method of inhibiting HTRAl protease activity in an eye comprising administering to a subject in need thereof a therapeutically effective amount of a compound according to Claims 1-11 or a pharmaceutically acceptable salt, solvate, solvate of the salt or prodrug thereof or the pharmaceutical composition of Claim 12.
